title: geum rivale subvar. grandifolium
intro: Geum rivale subvar. grandifolium, also known as the water avens, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps in reducing swelling and alleviating symptoms of various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits, such as promoting digestive health, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system. Geum rivale subvar. grandifolium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions like kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin irritations.
